analogy: Think of "Acids Bases and Salts" through the chapter's own opening images: 5 E very day you come across many changes in your surroundings. These changes may involve one or more substances. For example, your mother may ask you to dissolve sugar in water to make a cold drink. Similarly, setting curd from milk is a change. The easy idea is that the visible surface is only the outside cover. The chapter keeps asking how we can know what is inside when no one can directly reach the deep interior. So the learner must read clues: rocks, mines, drilling, volcanic material, heat, gravity, magnetism and earthquake waves. Like solving a hidden-layer puzzle, each clue tells something about the crust, mantle, core and volcanic activity without inventing anything beyond the textbook.
full_notes:
Acids Bases and Salts is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins by asking how the nature of the earth should be imagined and why the interior cannot be known in the same direct way as the surface. It connects the hidden interior with visible results on the surface, especially landscape development, earthquakes, tsunamis and volcanic activity.

The text explains that 5 E very day you come across many changes in your surroundings.

The text explains that these changes may involve one or more substances.

The text explains that for example, your mother may ask you to dissolve sugar in water to make a cold drink.

The text explains that similarly, setting curd from milk is a change.

The text explains that stretched rubber band also represents a change.

The text explains that make a list of ten changes you have noticed around you.

The text explains that in this chapter we shall perform some activities and study the nature of these changes.

The text explains that broadly, these changes are of two kinds, physical and chemical.

The text explains that 5.1 Paper pieces 5.1 P HYSICAL C HANGES Activity 5.1 Cut a piece of paper in four square pieces.

The text explains that cut each square piece further into four square pieces.

The text explains that lay these pieces on the floor or a table so that the pieces acquire the shape of the original piece of paper (Fig.

The text explains that obviously, you cannot join the pieces back to make the original piece, but is there a change in the property of the paper?

The text explains that add a little water to the dust to make a paste.

The text explains that melt a small portion of ice by placing the tumbler in the sun.

The text explains that now place the tumbler in a freezing mixture (ice plus common salt).

The text explains that sCIENCE 48 5.3 and 5.4, water changed its state (from solid to liquid, or from gas to liquid).

The text explains that fill bottle B with water which has been boiled for several minutes, to the same level as in A.

The text explains that in bottle C, take the same boiled water and of the same amount as in other bottles.

The text explains that in each bottle put a few similar iron nails so that they are com- pletely under water.

The text explains that add a teaspoonful of cooking oil to the water in bottle C so that it forms a film on its surface.

The text explains that take out nails from each bottle and observe them.

The text explains that collect information about the types of fuels used for cooking in your area.

The text explains that discuss with your teachers/parents/others which fuels are less polluting and why.

The text explains that near the Qutub Minar in Delhi stands an iron pillar (Fig.

The text explains that for its quality of rust resistance it has been examined by scientists from all parts of the world.

The text explains that it tells something about the advances India had made in metal technology as back as 1600 years ago.

Another source point is that 5 E very day you come across many changes in your surroundings.

Another source point is that these changes may involve one or more substances.

Another source point is that for example, your mother may ask you to dissolve sugar in water to make a cold drink.

Another source point is that similarly, setting curd from milk is a change.

Another source point is that stretched rubber band also represents a change.

Source: NCERT Class 7 Science, Chapter 5: Acids Bases and Salts

prelims_traps: Do not treat 5.1 P HYSICAL C HANGES and SCIENCE 48 as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Acids Bases and Salts; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain Acids Bases and Salts through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use 5.1 P HYSICAL C HANGES and SCIENCE 48 to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.
